scenario: Forge Manufacturing routes 1800 tasks per week to Tasklist in Camunda 8.8 for quality checks on the production line. A line lead opens the Tasks page and wants to sort the queue so the items he should start working on next, according to the latest planned start time, appear at the top of the list.
question: Which Tasklist ordering option matches the line lead goal in Camunda 8?

options.text: Order the queue by follow-up date; this date defines the latest time a Tasklist user should start working on a task according to the documented ordering options
Order the queue by due date, which provides the deadline for when the task should be finished according to the documented ordering options available in Tasklist
Order the queue by priority, which defines the urgency of a task in relation to other tasks waiting in the same Tasklist queue for assignment to a user
Order the queue by creation date, which lists the oldest tasks first and does not represent any planned start time for the assignee in the documented ordering options

a.text: Correct. The Tasklist ordering page states the follow-up date defines the latest time a user should start working on a task, which matches the line lead goal exactly.

b.text: Incorrect. Due date represents the deadline for when the task should be finished, not the latest planned start time, so it does not match the documented start-time signal.

c.text: Incorrect. Priority orders tasks by relative urgency among each other, but it does not encode a planned start date; two equally urgent tasks may have different follow-up dates.

d.text: Incorrect. Creation date orders the queue by task age, which has no relation to a planned start time and is not the documented ordering option for when work should begin.

explanation: Tasklist can order the queue by creation date, due date, follow-up date, or priority. The follow-up date specifically defines the latest time a user should start working on a task.
